title: IMD extends yellow heat alert for Mumbai, Thane and Raigad - Mid-day
search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Maharashtra
full_text:
Mumbaikars shield themselves amid the hot weather conditions in the city. PICS/BY SAYYED SAMEER ABEDI
Extending the heat wave warning for Mumbai and adjoining areas, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) on Wednesday issued a yellow alert for another day, indicating hot and humid weather conditions in isolated pockets.
The weather forecast agency had on Monday issued a heat warning for the city even as Mumbai experienced hot and humid weather in the past three days (Monday, Tuesday, and Wednesday), with temperatures going above 36Â°C. The yellow alert has also been extended to Thane and Raigad districts for Wednesday and Thursday.
Mainly clear skies. Hot and humid conditions are likely to prevail at isolated places in the city and suburbs. Maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 36Â°C and 24Â°C, respectively.
Forecast for Friday: Mainly clear skies in the city and suburbs. Maximum and minimum temperatures are likely to be around 34Â°C and 23Â°C, respectively.
